title: Missile-Maker Destinus Launches Short-Range Interceptor Program
summary: Destinus, the European missile startup, said it was developing a low-cost air defense interceptor aimed at defeating inexpensive but devastating weapons such as rocket artillery.

title: Tech Disruptors: Lazard’s Bilicic on Power Demand in the AI Era
summary: “The searches that you’re doing on Claude require the consumption of energy and at some point the system should hit a breaking point,” says George Bilicic, Lazard vice chairman of investment banking and global head of power, energy and infrastructure. Bilicic joins Bloomberg Intelligence’s Alexandra Davidov and Rob Barnett on this episode of the Tech Disruptors podcast to discuss why AI and data-center demand are reshaping the outlook for utilities, power markets and infrastructure investment. title: Samsung Biologics to Buy PolyPeptide in $1.8 Billion Deal
summary: Samsung Biologics Co. agreed to acquire Switzerland’s PolyPeptide Group AG in an all-cash deal that values the contract drugmaker’s equity at about 1.46 billion Swiss francs ($1.8 billion), expanding its manufacturing capabilities.

title: Stocks Climb as Chipmakers Bounce, Oil Fluctuates: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks are getting a lift from a rebound in chipmakers at the start of a week in which Big Tech earnings will help determine whether recent volatility persists or momentum returns. Oil swung between gains to losses.

title: Short Bets Against US Equities Hit Record as AI Risks Mount
summary: Bearish bets across US stocks are surging to a record in the face of market gains, reflecting anxiety about the staying power of a rally that has seen the S&P 500 jump 18% from late March.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 20
summary: Oil swung sharply between gains and losses in a fraught session as traders grappled with a barrage of headlines that suggest a worsening of the Middle East conflict, as well as attempts to bring about a pause in the war.

title: Reformation, Backers Seek $239 Million in US IPO
summary: Reformation Inc., a sustainable womenswear brand, and shareholders including its private equity backer Permira are seeking to raise as much as $239 million in its US initial public offering.

title: Hormuz Crisis Flares Just as Fuel Markets Tighten
summary: With the US summer driving season well underway, gasoline pump prices have again surged past $4 a gallon.

title: US Gasoline Tops $4 a Gallon Again as Iran War Escalates
summary: US gasoline prices at the pump climbed back above the $4-a-gallon mark as the Middle East conflict intensified, threatening to exacerbate a global crunch for transportation fuels and stoke inflation.

title: Jersey Mike’s, Backers Seek Up to $1.09 Billion in US IPO
summary: Sandwich chain Jersey Mike’s Subs Inc. and its shareholders are looking to raise as much as $1.09 billion in an initial public offering, as consumer-oriented companies join the US listing rebound.

title: South Korea Stock Boom: Why Kospi Is World’s Most Volatile Benchmark Stock Index
summary: Investing in South Korea’s stock market has yielded huge returns this year, but it’s been a wild ride. Volatility on the benchmark Kospi Index has topped 60%, almost double that of Japan’s Nikkei 225. It’s even put the notoriously skittish cryptocurrency Bitcoin in the shade.

title: Emerging Stocks Extend Drop on Energy, Semiconductor Jitters
summary: Emerging equities extended losses as renewed US bombing of Iran lifted oil prices above $90 a barrel and chip stocks remained under pressure.

title: KKR-Backed Serentica Eyes $450 Million Loan in Renewables Push
summary: Serentica Renewables India Pvt Ltd., backed by private equity firm KKR & Co., is raising about $450 million through an offshore loan, underscoring growing global appetite to fund India’s clean energy expansion.

title: Key China Copper Gauge Rallies to $100 After Tax Crackdown
summary: A closely-watched gauge of China’s copper market has risen to the highest in more than a year, as a tax shake-up in the metal’s biggest market spurs a shortage of scrap and boosts demand for imports.

title: Alkegen Plans Prepackaged Bankruptcy in Deal for Fresh Cash
summary: Alkegen has signed a restructuring deal with lenders that will inject the specialty materials maker with new money and slash its debt load by about $3.1 billion, according to a person familiar with the situation.

title: Singapore Mulls Lower Taxes, Easier Talent Entry for Hedge Funds
summary: Singapore is considering cutting taxes for hedge funds and other asset managers and making it easier for them to hire more foreign talent, as rival Hong Kong nears finalization of widespread tax exemptions, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Philippine Senate Seeks VP Duterte’s Bank, Tax Records in Trial
summary: The Philippine Senate impeachment court will subpoena the bank and tax records of Vice President Sara Duterte as her opponents seek to oust the nation’s second-highest official on allegations that include corruption and unexplained wealth.

title: Big Tech Needs to Prove that AI Spending Is Going Somewhere
summary: Alphabet, Tesla kick off earnings for the sector this week.

title: SBI Funds Pays Razor-Thin Banker Fees on Top India IPO of 2026
summary: The nine investment banks that manage SBI Funds Management Ltd.‘s initial public offering, India‘s first billion-dollar debut this year, will receive one of the worst payouts on a large debut in the country.

title: Citi Says Magnificent Seven Tag Is Obsolete in AI Stock Winners
summary: The popular Magnificent Seven stocks moniker is “no longer relevant” in assessing how to play the US artificial intelligence trade, according to Citigroup Inc. strategists.

title: South Korea Details Plan to Make Won Freely Tradable Abroad
summary: South Korea laid out a detailed plan to make the won freely tradable among foreigners, its boldest step yet to liberalize the foreign-exchange market.

title: Gold Steadies as Traders Assess Rates Path Amid US-Iran Conflict
summary: Gold steadied after escalating US-Iran clashes over the weekend were followed by reports of mediation efforts, with traders assessing the likelihood of the Federal Reserve raising interest rates to contain inflation.

title: Segro Rejects £13.5 Billion Cash and Share Prologis Offer
summary: Prologis Inc. has increased its outline bid to buy Segro Plc to £13.5 billion ($18.2 billion) and offered a series of sweeteners as it seeks to tempt shareholders after the UK landlord’s board rejected the latest terms.
